channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
item.description: Ukraine launched a new wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday, amounting to one of the largest attacks on the Russian capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine launched a new wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday, amounting to one of the largest attacks on the Russian capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: You're probably using too many skin care products. Here are the 3 essentials
item.description: We asked half a dozen skin care experts: Which products do you really need to keep your skin healthy and attractive? Here's what they said.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

We asked half a dozen skin care experts: Which products do you really need to keep your skin healthy and attractive? Here's what they said.
item.dc:creator: Kelle Walsh

item.title: These 3 brothers lost their parents to AIDS. Now they struggle to make it on their own
item.description: Three brothers say their mother and father died after losing access to their HIV medications. Now the boys are figuring out how to navigate life.
item.content:encoded:
Three brothers say their mother and father died after losing access to their HIV medications. Now the boys are figuring out how to navigate life.

(Image credit: Andy Higgins for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Supreme Court sides with a marijuana user who was barred from owning guns
item.description: The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.
item.content:encoded:
The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: Embedded: "We Keep Us Safe" from NPR, KUOW and The Seattle Times
item.description: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.
item.content:encoded: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.

item.title: Report: Russia's nuclear-powered 'Skyfall' missile is dirty and dangerous
item.description: MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."
item.content:encoded:
MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."

(Image credit: Russian Ministry of Defense)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Trump signs agreement with Iran. And, the president's approval hits record lows
item.description: Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.
item.content:encoded:
Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The Obama Presidential Center will be dedicated Thursday. Here's what to expect
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony will be a star-studded event. The center's museum highlights the legacy of the former president, but it is not a traditional presidential library.
item.content:encoded:
The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony will be a star-studded event. The center's museum highlights the legacy of the former president, but it is not a traditional presidential library.

(Image credit: Pablo Martinez Monsivais)
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid

item.title: As America turns 250, one museum makes history possible to touch
item.description: Federal law requires most museums and other buildings to be accessible to people with disabilities. But access to what's actually inside is often still limited.
item.content:encoded:
Federal law requires most museums and other buildings to be accessible to people with disabilities. But access to what's actually inside is often still limited.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: What — and who — will be at the Great American State Fair? Here's a primer
item.description: Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.
item.content:encoded:
Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
